value.body:
## Problem

The frontend does not make it clear whether a ticket chat is the managed implementation thread or an ordinary read-only conversation. Users cannot reliably see the worktree, branch, permission mode, last handed-over commit, or whether follow-up changes were pushed.

This makes context downgrades and publication failures appear as unpredictable agent behavior.

## Desired behavior

Ticket chat should visibly present the durable implementation context and its current lifecycle. A human reopening the chat can confirm that feedback will run in the same full-permission worktree before sending it.

## Implementation detail

- Extend the public thread/task resource with a redacted implementation-context projection:
  - context kind and workflow
  - repository and ticket
  - managed worktree ID and display path
  - branch name
  - authority/runtime mode
  - provider/model and resumable-session status
  - task state including finalizing/publish-blocked
  - last handed-over commit
  - remote branch and last successful push time
  - dirty/unpublished/cleanup-blocked summary from authoritative backend projections
- Add an implementation-context header/card to ticket chat.
- Clearly distinguish:
  - Managed implementation
  - Read-only ticket context
  - General conversation
  - Context unavailable/recovery required
- Before sending feedback, use the existing implementation thread returned by the ticket API. If none exists, offer an explicit "Start implementation" action rather than silently creating a chat with a different authority.
- Disable or block feedback submission when the implementation context is incomplete, removed, or points to a different ticket/repository; provide Resume, Recover, or Start new implementation actions as appropriate.
- Show publishing progress after provider output: Finalizing, Committing, Pushing, Published, Publish failed.
- Link the branch/commit where resolvable and show a copyable branch name.
- Display normalized permission and workflow errors without exposing tokens, environment variables, raw provider payloads, or unsafe filesystem details.
- Keep UI state driven by the canonical API/task/worktree resources and websocket events; do not infer authority from the chat title or prompt text.

## Acceptance criteria

- A reopened implementation chat visibly shows the same ticket, worktree, branch, and authority as the initial assignment.
- Users can distinguish implementation and read-only conversations before sending a message.
- Follow-up publishing progress and final commit/push result update live.
- Missing/stale context blocks submission with an actionable recovery path.
- The UI never labels a generic `conversation-read` thread as implementation merely because its title starts with "Work on".
- Branch and publish information survives reload and frontend reconnection.
- Sensitive configuration is redacted.

## Tests

- Renderer tests for each context kind and lifecycle state.
- Reconnect/reload test preserving the implementation thread and header data.
- End-to-end test from ticket assignment through publish, human feedback, second publish, and review.
- Accessibility coverage for status announcements, disabled-action reasons, and keyboard navigation.
- Contract tests ensuring implementation metadata is redacted and schema validated.

## Non-goals

- A general-purpose Git branch manager.
- Exposing credentials or raw sandbox configuration.

frontmatter.title: Expose implementation context, permissions, and publish state in ticket chat UI

value.title: Expose implementation context, permissions, and publish state in ticket chat UI
